name: Learn Java
description: Learn to code in Java — a robust programming language used to create software, web and mobile apps, and more.

modules.title: Hello World
modules.description: Welcome to the world of Java programming! Java is a popular object-oriented programming language that is used in many different industries.

lessons.title: Introduction to Java

content.value: HELLO WORLD
Introduction to Java
3 min
Welcome to the world of Java programming!
Programming languages enable humans to write instructions that a computer can perform. With precise instructions, computers coordinate applications and systems that run the modern world.

Sun Microsystems released the Java programming language in 1995. Java is known for being simple, portable, secure, and robust. Though it was released over twenty years ago, Java remains one of the most popular programming languages today.

One reason people love Java is the Java Virtual Machine, which ensures the same Java code can be run on different operating systems and platforms. Sun Microsystems' slogan for Java was "write once, run everywhere".
/assets/java_jvm_diagram.png
content.caption: Java Virtual Machine running Java on three different platforms

content.value: Programming languages are composed of syntax, the specific instructions which Java understands. We write syntax in files to create programs, which are executed by the computer to perform the desired task.
Let's start with the universal greeting for a programming language. We'll explore the syntax in the next exercise.
Instructions

You're looking at a computer program written in Java.

Run the code in the text editor to see what is printed to the screen.
Concept Review
Want to quickly review some of the concepts you've been learning? Take a look at this material's cheatsheet!
Community Support
Still have questions? Get help from the community.

lessons.title: Java Program Structure

content.value: HELLO WORLD
Java Program Structure
5 min
Every Java program starts with a class. The class is a blueprint for creating objects and contains all the code for the program.
Inside the class, we define the main method. This is the entry point of any Java application. When you run a Java program, the JVM looks for this method and starts executing from here.

The keyword public means the class or method is accessible from anywhere. The keyword static means the method belongs to the class itself, not an instance of the class. The keyword void means the method does not return any value.
String[] args is a parameter that passes command-line arguments into the program. You can ignore it for now.
Key Takeaways
Every Java program has at least one class.
The main method is the entry point of every Java program.
public, static, and void are important Java keywords.

lessons.title: Hello World Quiz

content.value: HELLO WORLD
Hello World Quiz
5 min
Test your knowledge of the Hello World lesson with this quick quiz.
Question 1
What year was Java released by Sun Microsystems?
Question 2
What is the slogan associated with the Java Virtual Machine?
Question 3
What method is the entry point of a Java application?

lessons.title: Planting a Tree

content.value: HELLO WORLD
Planting a Tree
20 min
In this project, you will use your knowledge of print statements to create ASCII art of a tree!
ASCII art is a graphic design technique that uses printable characters from the ASCII character set to display images or create text-based designs.
Instructions
Using System.out.println() statements, create the trunk of your tree using | characters.
Create the leaves of your tree using * characters in a triangular shape.
Add a message below the tree like "I planted a tree!".

lessons.title: What Is an IDE?

content.value: HELLO WORLD
What Is an IDE?
4 min
An Integrated Development Environment (IDE) is a software application that provides comprehensive facilities for software development.
A typical IDE consists of: a source code editor, build automation tools, and a debugger. Most modern IDEs also include intelligent code completion.
Popular Java IDEs include IntelliJ IDEA, Eclipse, and NetBeans. They make writing, testing, and debugging Java programs much easier than using a plain text editor.
Popular IDEs for Java
IntelliJ IDEA — The most popular Java IDE, made by JetBrains.
Eclipse — A free, open-source IDE widely used in enterprise.
VS Code — Lightweight editor with Java extension support.

modules.title: Variables
modules.description: Learn about datatypes in Java and how we use them. Then, practice your skills with two projects where you create and manipulate variables.

lessons.title: Learn Java: Variables

content.value: VARIABLES
Learn Java: Variables
8 min
A variable is a named location in memory that stores a value. In Java, every variable must be declared with a data type.
Java is a statically typed language, which means you must declare the type of a variable before using it. Common data types include:
int — Stores whole numbers (e.g., 42, -7)
double — Stores decimal numbers (e.g., 3.14)
String — Stores text (e.g., "Hello")
boolean — Stores true or false
In the example above, we declare four variables with different data types. The variable name comes after the type, followed by an = sign and the variable's value.
Instructions
Declare an int variable called score and set it to 100.
Declare a String variable called playerName and set it to your name.
Print both variables using System.out.println().

modules.title: Object-Oriented Java
modules.description: Learn about object-oriented programming in Java. Explore syntax for defining classes and creating instances.

lessons.title: Java: Introduction to Classes

content.value: OBJECT-ORIENTED JAVA
Java: Introduction to Classes
10 min

In object-oriented programming, a class is a blueprint from which individual objects are created. A class defines the properties (instance variables) and behaviors (methods) that its objects will have.
Think of a class like a cookie cutter and objects like the cookies. The cookie cutter defines the shape; the cookies are the actual items created from that mold.
Instructions
Create a class called Bicycle with instance variables color and gears.
Add a constructor that sets these variables.
Add a method called ride() that prints "Riding the [color] bicycle!".
In the main method, create a Bicycle object and call ride().

modules.title: Loops
modules.description: Use loops to iterate through lists and repeat code.

lessons.title: Learn Java: Loops

content.value: LOOPS
Learn Java: Loops
8 min
A loop is a programming construct that repeats a block of code until a condition is met. Java has three types of loops: for, while, and do-while.
The for loop is used when you know exactly how many times you want to repeat a block. The while loop is used when you want to repeat a block as long as a condition is true.
Instructions
Write a for loop that prints the numbers 1 through 10.
Write a while loop that prints even numbers from 2 to 20.
